content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
July 27. 1988 
James Bratt, Treasurer 
CA Republican Assembly Membership 
Action committee 
5232 Butterwood Circle 
Orangevale, CA 
95662 
Dear Mr. Bratt: 
RE: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-88-235 
You have requested advice under the campaign disclosure 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the ItActlt) • .lI 
FACTS 
I 
In September 1984, you took over the duties of treasurer 
for the California Republican Assembly Membership Action 
Committee (CRAMAC). 
In October 1984, CRAMAC made two payments 
to the Friends of John Doolittle Committee. 
The two payments 
were made conditionally. 
If John Doolittle was elected, the 
Friends of John Doolittle Committee would reimburse CRAMAC for 
the payments it made in October, 1984. 
A loan agreement was 
drafted but never signed. 
At the time the two payments were 
made by CRAMAC, CRAMAC's previous treasurer advised you that, 
because no loan agreement was signed, the payments should be 
reported as monetary contributions and at some later date, when 
the loan agreement was signed, the payments would be reported 
by CRAMAC as loans to the Friends of John Doolittle Committee. 
No loan agreement was ever signed. 
On June 17, 1988, CRAMAC 
received a check in the amount of $3,000 from the Friends of 
John Doolittle Committee as reimbursement for the two payments. 
QUESTION 
How is the receipt of the $3,000 from the Friends of John 
Doolittle Committee reported by CRAMAC? 
CONCLUSION 
CRAMAC will report the $3,000 as an increase to cash on 
Schedule G. CRAMAC should also explain on Schedule G the 
circumstances surrounding the receipt. 
lIGovernment Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code 
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code 
of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


James Bratt 
Page 2 
ANALYSIS 
The Act does not require committees to enter into loan 
agreements. 
Loan agreements are discretionary. 
The facts presented in your letter indicate that the 
payments were intended to be a loan and not a monetary 
contribution to the Friends of John Doolittle committee. 
During our telephone conversation of July 18, 1988, you advised 
me that there was a misunderstanding between CREMAC and the 
Friends of John Doolittle Committee regarding the nature of the 
contribution. 
I advised you to report the repayment as an 
increase to cash on Schedule G of CRAMAC's Form 420 and provide 
an explanation of the facts as stated in your letter of June 
19, 1988. 
Please do not hesitate to contact me at 916/322-5662 if 
you have any additional questions concerning this matter. 
DMG:MAK:kmt 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
( 
By: 
Mary An 
Kvasager 
Political Reform Consultant
